detail: The area that would become Greenville was first settled and established as Greenville Station, a stop along early Wisconsin transportation routes.
The community's name was changed to Becker, reflecting changes in the local community identity.
The community adopted the name Town of Greenville, formalizing its municipal identity under Wisconsin's township government structure.
After years of rapid growth, the community voted to incorporate as the Village of Greenville under Wisconsin statutes, transitioning from town government to village governance.
